questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is the tallest of the two sisters.
options: She is the taller of the two sisters.
She is the tallest of the two sisters.
She is more tall of the two sisters.
She is tall among the two sisters.
questions.solution: She is the taller of the two sisters.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Hardly had we left ____ it started raining.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I prefer coffee than tea.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The city is known ____ its coal deposits.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The police has arrested the suspect last night.
options: The police have arrested the suspect last night.
The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She was so tired ____ she fell asleep immediately.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
options: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.
She is the most talented singer in the industry.
questions.solution: She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She speaks ____ softly that no one can hear her.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
options: I would rather tea than coffee.
I would rather have tea than coffee.
I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
I prefer tea rather than coffee.
questions.solution: I would rather have tea than coffee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the rain, the match was cancelled.

questions.stem: Correct: The informations provided is accurate.
options: The information provided has been accurate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is capable ____ doing the job independently.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
options: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
A pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pair of shoes are kept in the cupboard.
The pairs of shoes is kept in the cupboard.
questions.solution: The pair of shoes is kept in the cupboard.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ intelligent that he solves problems instantly.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
options: The professor gave a one hour lecture.
The professor gave a one-hour lecture.
questions.solution: The professor gave a one-hour lecture.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ you work hard, you will not succeed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Hardly had he arrived than the meeting started.
options: He had hardly arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived then the meeting started.
Hardly he had arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.
questions.solution: Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.

She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Blocked rotor test measures:
questions.solution: Blocked: reduced V for rated I. P ≈ Cu loss (referred to stator).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eddy current loss:
questions.solution: Eddy current: induced currents in core. Laminations increase resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a first-order RC low-pass filter, which statement is correct?
options: It passes high frequencies and blocks low ones.
Above the cutoff frequency the gain rolls off at 20 dB/decade.
questions.solution: A first-order low-pass passes low frequencies; cutoff fc = 1/(2πRC) and roll-off is 20 dB/decade (single pole).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase sequence in 3-phase:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power quality issues:
options: Sag, swell, interruption, transient, harmonic, notch, flicker
questions.solution: Sag: voltage dip (0.1-0.9 pu). Swell: voltage rise.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PI controller:
options: Eliminates steady-state error, reduces stability margin
questions.solution: PI: adds pole at origin + zero. Ziegler-Nichols tuning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earth pit construction:
questions.solution: Charcoal reduces soil resistivity. Salt provides electrolyte. Water maintains conductivity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 25 A at 440 V line, pf 0.8. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: THD:
questions.solution: IEEE 519: THD < 5% for general systems. Individual harmonic < 3%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 200 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root locus:
options: Plot of closed-loop poles as K varies from 0 to ∞
questions.solution: RL: starts at open-loop poles, ends at zeros or infinity. Rules: asymptotes, breakaway.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona discharge on a transmission line increases with:
questions.solution: Corona worsens as surface gradient rises (higher voltage, thinner or rough conductors, bad weather).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Load frequency control:
options: Maintains frequency constant by balancing generation and load
questions.solution: Governor: primary control. AGC: secondary (restores frequency and tie-line power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: String efficiency:
questions.solution: η_s = V_total / (n×V_disc). Improved by grading rings or longer cross-arms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Neutral grounding:
options: Solid, resistance, reactance, or Peterson coil
questions.solution: Solid: high fault current. Resistance: limits fault current. Peterson: arc suppression.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 100 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: No-load test measures:
questions.solution: No-load: rated V, no load. Input ≈ core + friction + windage losses.

questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.
